返回本批次

卓越是一种习惯

Excellence Is a Habit

www.flyingbarron.com 作者 mooreds 分数 27 评论 3 2026/04/12 11:34
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. “我们就是我们反复做的事情。那么，卓越不是一种行为，而是一种习惯。” ——历史学家威尔·杜兰特，简化了亚里士多德的部分哲学。
  2. 当我写下这些文字时，阿耳忒弥斯二号的船员们已经安全、成功地返回地球，这是 50 多年来第一批到达月球附近的人类。这也是阿波罗 13 号发射 56 周年，这项任务不仅因登月途中发生的灾难性事件而闻名，更因最终让宇航员成功返回家园而付出的巨大努力而闻名。 Artemis II landing, April 11th 2026 (NASA)
  3. NASA 在 60 年代和 70 年代初的登月计划是一步一步完成的漫长旅程的故事。简单的单人水星飞船为两人双子座飞船铺平了道路，双子座飞船是双阿波罗太空舱和月球着陆器的垫脚石。 1961 年 5 月至 1970 年 4 月期间，NASA 发射了 25 次载人任务，任务间隔平均约为 4-5 个月，在 1965-66 年达到顶峰，任务间隔仅 1-2 个月。
  4. 美国宇航局宇航员、工程师和管理人员是这台运转良好的机器的一部分，这台机器不仅实现了肯尼迪总统按计划着陆（并使其安全返回地球）的目标，而且还成功实现了五次。这台机器在实践中经过了压力测试，因此能够拯救命运多舛的阿波罗13号，从一场悲剧中夺取了一个胜利的英雄故事。
  5. 当今的许多软件开发和弹性概念都是围绕同一个理念构建的，即您对流程的练习越多，您就能越好地使用它。这就是为什么 DevOps 持续交付管道会自动执行可重复的部署任务，以便在正常的日子里向客户交付新功能就像往常一样，而不是充满惊喜的令人紧张的体验。这也是为什么基础设施即代码是一个如此引人注目的概念，因为它既支持重复又具有灵活性。

Original Summary

  1. “We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.” -- Historian Will Durant, simplifying part of Aristotle’s philosophy.
  2. As I write these words, the crew of Artemis II has returned safely and successfully to Earth, after being the first humans to have reached the vicinity of the Moon in over 50 years. It is also the 56th anniversary of the launch of Apollo 13, a mission known not only for the catastrophic events on the way to the Moon, but even more so for the Herculean efforts made to eventually return the astronauts successfully back home. Artemis II landing, April 11th 2026 (NASA)
  3. NASA’s foray to the Moon in the 60s and early 70s is the story of a long journey made step-by-step. The simple one-man Mercury spacecraft paved the way to the two-man Gemini craft which was a stepping stone to the twin Apollo space capsule and Moon lander. Between May of 1961 and April of 1970, NASA launched twenty five manned missions, a cadence with an average of around 4-5 months between missions, peaking in 1965-66 with only 1-2 months between missions.
  4. NASA astronauts, engineers, and managers were part of a well-oiled machine which not only achieved President Kennedy’s goal of landing a man (and returning him safely to Earth) on schedule but did it successfully five more times. The machine had been tested under stress in practice and was therefore able to save the ill-fated Apollo 13, snatching a heroic story of the triumph of achievement out of what could well have been a tragedy.
  5. Many of today’s concepts of software development and resilience are built around the same idea that the more you practice a process, the better you can become with it. That’s why DevOps continuous delivery pipelines automate repeatable deployment tasks so that delivering a new feature to clients is just business as usual on a normal day instead of a nail-biting experience, fraught with surprises. This is also why Infrastructure-as-code is such a compelling concept, because it enables both repetition and flexibility.

评论（默认中文）

boca_honey 2026/04/12 13:27
> 如果您想了解更多关于我如何帮助我的客户实现这一切的信息，请随时与我们联系。

这是一个很长很长的广告。

> If you’d like to learn more about how I help my clients achieve all this, please feel free to reach out.

This is a long, long winded ad.

在 HN 中查看
macrocosmos 2026/04/12 13:51
我几乎总是阅读一篇文章的第一句话，然后是最后一段，然后是完整的第一段，然后是中间段落的开头句子，直到最后阅读整篇文章。很多时候，在这个过程中我会放弃这件作品。

我很感激一篇文章能如此清楚地告诉我的思绪这么快就停下来。不管怎样，我点击了这篇文章，看到了你链接的那一行，点击了评论，并很高兴看到其他人指出了那一行。

I pretty much always read the first sentence of an article, then the last paragraph, and then the full first paragraph, then the beginning sentences of middle paragraphs, until finally reading an entire piece. Often times, along this process I'll abandon the piece.

I appreciate when an article can so clearly tell my mind to stop so soon. Anyways, I clicked on this article, saw that line you linked, clicked the comments and was pleased somehow to see someone else point that line out.

在 HN 中查看
d--b 2026/04/12 13:15
应该叫韧性是一种习惯。卓越是另一回事，而且绝对不是一种习惯。
Should be called Resiliance is a habit. Excellence is something else, and definitely not a habit.
